question_text: A 1500 kg car accelerates at 2 m/s² while being pulled by an engine force of 5000 N. Using F = ma, calculate the total resistance forces (friction and air resistance) acting against the car's motion.
correct_answer: Net force needed = ma = 1500 × 2 = 3000 N. Engine force - resistance forces = net force. Therefore: 5000 - resistance = 3000. Resistance forces = 5000 - 3000 = 2000 N.
explanation: Newton's second law tells us the net (resultant) force needed to cause acceleration. By rearranging the equation, we can find the unknown resistance forces acting against the motion.
